questions.audioText: Woman: So what happened after you missed the bus? Man: I decided to walk to the station, but it started raining halfway. By the time I got there, I was completely soaked. The next train wasn't for another hour. Woman: What a terrible morning!
questions.question: Why did the man get wet?
options: He forgot his umbrella at home
It started raining while he walked
He took a shower with his clothes on

questions.audioText: Lecturer: One common mistake language learners make is translating directly from their native language. Instead, try to think in the new language. It might feel strange at first, but it will improve your fluency significantly over time.
questions.question: What advice does the lecturer give to language learners?

questions.audioText: Woman: I read an article about how sleep affects our memory. Apparently, when you sleep, your brain organizes and stores what you learned during the day. Man: That explains why I remember things better after a good night's sleep.
questions.question: According to the article, what does the brain do during sleep?

questions.audioText: Man: I'm thinking about taking up photography as a hobby. Woman: That's a great idea! You've always had a good eye for detail. You should start with a basic camera, though, not something too expensive.
questions.question: What does the woman suggest about starting photography?

questions.audioText: Radio host: Welcome back. Today we're discussing the rise of electric vehicles. Our guest says that while they are better for the environment, the batteries are still difficult to recycle. There's also the question of charging infrastructure in rural areas.
questions.question: What problem with electric vehicles does the guest mention?

questions.audioText: Woman: I can't believe Jake got promoted. He's only been with the company for six months. Man: True, but he works harder than anyone I know. He's always the first to arrive and the last to leave.
questions.question: Why does the man think Jake got promoted?

questions.audioText: Woman: We need to book our flights soon. Prices are going up every week. Man: I know, but I'm waiting to see if my vacation request gets approved first. Woman: You should ask your boss today then.
questions.question: Why hasn't the man booked his flight yet?
options: He is waiting for vacation approval
He prefers to travel by train
He hasn't decided on a destination

questions.audioText: Professor: The Industrial Revolution transformed society in many ways. Cities grew rapidly as people moved from rural areas for factory work. Living conditions were often poor, but it marked the beginning of modern urban life as we know it.
questions.question: What caused cities to grow during the Industrial Revolution?

questions.audioText: Woman: I've been feeling tired lately, even after a full night's sleep. Man: Maybe you should check your iron levels. My sister had the same problem, and it turned out she was anemic.
questions.question: What does the man suggest might be the cause of the woman's tiredness?

questions.audioText: Man: I don't understand why people still send letters. Email is so much faster. Woman: There's something special about receiving a handwritten letter, though. It feels more personal, more meaningful.
questions.question: What is the woman's opinion about handwritten letters?
options: They are a waste of paper
